---
description: Frontend development guidelines for Boreal DS. Load these instructions when working on Stencil web components, SCSS styles, design tokens, Storybook stories, MDX documentation, or unit tests inside the monorepo.
applyTo: "packages/boreal-web-components/**,apps/boreal-docs/**,packages/boreal-styleguidelines/**"
---

# Frontend Instructions ‚Äî Boreal DS

## Overview

Boreal DS is a multi-brand design system for Proximus Group (brands: Proximus, Masiv, Telesign, BICS). It is built on Stencil web components published as framework-agnostic custom elements, with auto-generated React and Vue wrappers. All visual consistency is enforced through a three-layer design token system using CSS custom properties.

These instructions apply to all component implementation work: Stencil components, SCSS styles, unit tests, Storybook stories, and MDX documentation.

---

## Technology Stack

### Core Technologies

| Technology                | Version             | Role                                            |
| ------------------------- | ------------------- | ----------------------------------------------- |
| **TypeScript**            | 5.x                 | All source files; no plain JavaScript in `src/` |
| **Stencil**               | 4.x                 | Web component compiler and runtime              |
| **SCSS**                  | via `@stencil/sass` | Component styles                                |
| **CSS Custom Properties** | native              | Multi-brand runtime theming (`var(--boreal-*)`) |
| **Node.js**               | 22+                 | Runtime; managed via `fnm`                      |
| **pnpm**                  | 10+                 | Workspace package manager                       |

### UI Framework

Boreal DS is **the** UI framework ‚Äî it does not depend on React, Vue, or any external UI library. Each publishable package is:

- `@telesign/boreal-web-components` ‚Äî Stencil web components (the source of truth)
- `@telesign/boreal-react` ‚Äî React wrappers auto-generated by `@stencil/react-output-target`
- `@telesign/boreal-vue` ‚Äî Vue 3 wrappers auto-generated by `@stencil/vue-output-target`
- `@telesign/boreal-style-guidelines` ‚Äî Design tokens as CSS custom properties and SCSS variables

Do not introduce any third-party UI component library (e.g. Bootstrap, Radix, Material UI) as a dependency of any publishable package. The `apps/boreal-docs` Storybook app uses Lit 3.x exclusively for story render functions ‚Äî do not use React or Vue directly inside story files.

### State Management & Data Flow

Stencil components are the only stateful units in the stack. State flows in one direction:

```
Design tokens (CSS custom properties)
        ‚Üì
Stencil component internal @State / @Prop
        ‚Üì
Shadow DOM render output
        ‚Üì
Slotted consumer content
```

- `@Prop()` ‚Äî external, parent-controlled input (immutable inside the component)
- `@State()` ‚Äî internal reactive state (triggers re-render on change)
- `@Event()` ‚Äî output; notifies the consumer of user interactions or internal changes
- `@Method()` ‚Äî imperative API for actions that cannot be expressed as props (e.g. `focus()`, `open()`)

Never use global state managers (Redux, Zustand, Pinia, etc.) inside component packages.

### Testing Framework

| Scope         | Tool                                                    | Command                                                       |
| ------------- | ------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------- |
| Unit tests    | Jest 29 via `stencil test --spec`                       | `pnpm --filter @telesign/boreal-web-components run test:spec` |
| Utility tests | Vitest                                                  | `pnpm --filter @telesign/boreal-web-components run test`      |
| Integration   | Manual in `examples/react-testapp`                      | `pnpm dev:components`                                         |
| E2E           | Stencil + Puppeteer (configured in `testing.config.ts`) | `stencil test --e2e`                                          |

### Development Tools

| Tool                        | Role                                                  |
| --------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------- |
| **Turborepo**               | Task pipeline (build, lint, test across all packages) |
| **Storybook 10.x + Vite**   | Interactive component development and documentation   |
| **Plop.js**                 | Code generation for components and stories            |
| **ESLint 9.x flat config**  | Linting (`eslint.config.js` / `eslint.config.ts`)     |
| **Prettier 3.x**            | Formatting                                            |
| **Husky + lint-staged**     | Pre-commit quality gates                              |
| **commitizen + commitlint** | Conventional commit enforcement                       |

---

## Project Structure

```
boreal-ds/
‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ packages/
‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ boreal-web-components/          # @telesign/boreal-web-components
‚îÇ   ‚îÇ   ‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ src/
‚îÇ   ‚îÇ       ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ components/
‚îÇ   ‚îÇ       ‚îÇ   ‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ [category]/         # actions | feedback | display | navigation | ‚Ä¶

‚îÇ   ‚îÇ       ‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ utils/
‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ boreal-styleguidelines/         # @telesign/boreal-style-guidelines
‚îÇ   ‚îÇ   ‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ src/

‚îÇ   ‚îÇ       ‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ styles/                 # Global reset and shared SCSS
‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ boreal-react/                   # @telesign/boreal-react (auto-generated)
‚îÇ   ‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ boreal-vue/                     # @telesign/boreal-vue (auto-generated)
‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ apps/

‚îÇ       ‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ src/

‚îÇ           ‚îÇ       ‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ boreal-[name].mdx
‚îÇ           ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ components/
‚îÇ           ‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ docs/               # React 19 components for MDX pages only
‚îÇ           ‚îÇ   ‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ story/              # Lit 3 components for story Canvas only
‚îÇ           ‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ types/
‚îÇ               ‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ stories.ts          # BorealStoryMeta, BorealStory generics
‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ examples/
    ‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ react-testapp/                  # Integration sandbox (not published)
```

---

## Coding Standards

### Language and Naming Conventions

- All source files must be TypeScript 5.x. No plain JavaScript in `src/` directories.
- Prefer `interface` over `type` aliases for object shapes. Use `type` for unions, intersections, and utility types.
- Avoid `any`; use `unknown` or precise union types. Treat `@typescript-eslint/no-explicit-any` warnings as errors.
- Use the `satisfies` operator for Storybook meta objects: `export default { ... } satisfies BorealStoryMeta`.
- Variables prefixed with `_` are intentionally unused.

| Artifact                | Convention                   | Example                              |
| ----------------------- | ---------------------------- | ------------------------------------ |
| Web component directory | `kebab-case`                 | `src/components/actions/bds-button/` |
| Web component file      | `kebab-case`                 | `bds-button.tsx`                     |
| Web component tag       | `bds-[name]` in `kebab-case` | `<bds-button>`                       |
| SCSS file               | `kebab-case`                 | `bds-button.scss`                    |
| Test file               | `.spec.ts` suffix            | `bds-button.spec.ts`                 |
| Story file              | `kebab-case`                 | `bds-button.stories.ts`              |
| MDX file                | `kebab-case`                 | `bds-button.mdx`                     |
| Docs component (React)  | `PascalCase` folder + file   | `Callout/Callout.tsx`                |
| Story component (Lit)   | `PascalCase` folder + file   | `CodeBlock/CodeBlock.ts`             |

### Import Order

Imports in Stencil component files must follow this order:

1. **Framework** ‚Äî `@stencil/core` and any third-party `node_modules`
2. **Internal aliases** (`@/...`) ‚Äî ordered by abstraction layer, most abstract first:
   - `@/services` ‚Äî pure logic, no DOM dependency
   - `@/mixins` ‚Äî compose services into Stencil behaviour
   - `@/utils` ‚Äî constants and helpers
3. **Local/relative** (`./` or `../`) ‚Äî component-specific types, interfaces, assets

```tsx
import { Component, Host, Prop, h } from "@stencil/core";
import {
  PositioningResult,
  FloatingTooltipProp,
  AnchoredHooks,
} from "@/services";
import { anchoredMixin } from "@/mixins";
import { ANCHORED_TOOLTIP } from "@/utils";
import { ITooltip } from "./types/ITooltip";
```

The underlying principle: **dependencies flow downward**. Each group only imports from groups above it. Import order mirrors that dependency direction.

### Barrel Exports and Tree-Shaking

Internal barrel files (`@/services`, `@/mixins`, `@/utils`) are resolved at compile time by Stencil/Rollup and are safe for internal use. However, keep these rules to protect the published package's tree-shakability:

- **Only barrel-export what belongs to the contract** of that layer. Do not re-export every internal utility through `@/services` just for convenience ‚Äî hidden re-exports obscure coupling and can prevent dead code elimination.
- **Never use `export *` from a module that has side effects.** Rollup treats `export *` as a potential side-effect boundary and may retain the entire module in the bundle.
- **Prefer named re-exports** (`export { X } from './X'`) over wildcard re-exports (`export * from './X'`) in barrel files. Named exports give bundlers explicit edges to follow during tree-shaking.
- **Components never barrel-export each other.** The Stencil lazy-loading architecture splits each component into its own chunk automatically ‚Äî cross-component barrel imports defeat this.

These rules apply to internal (`@/`) barrels only. The published package entry point (`dist/index.js`) is managed by Stencil's output targets and must not be edited manually.

### Component Conventions

Every Stencil component must follow this structure:

```tsx
import { Component, Host, Prop, Event, EventEmitter, h } from "@stencil/core";

// 1. Export all custom union types at the top
export type ButtonVariant = "default" | "ghost" | "outline" | "flat";
export type ButtonSize = "small" | "medium" | "large";

@Component({
  tag: "bds-button", // bds- prefix, kebab-case
  styleUrl: "bds-button.scss",
})
export class BdsButton {
  // 2. Every @Prop must have: explicit type, JSDoc, and default value
  /** Visual variant of the button */
  @Prop() variant: ButtonVariant = "default";

  /** Disables the button */
  @Prop() disabled: boolean = false;

  // 3. @Event must always set bubbles and composed so events cross shadow DOM
  /** Emitted when the button is clicked */
  @Event({ bubbles: true, composed: true }) bdsClick!: EventEmitter<void>;

  // 4. Use a getter for dynamic BEM class strings ‚Äî not inline logic in render()
  private get hostClasses(): string {
    return [
      "bds-button",
      `bds-button--${this.variant}`,
      this.disabled ? "bds-button--disabled" : "",
    ]
      .filter(Boolean)
      .join(" ");
  }

  // 5. Render uses <Host> as root; ARIA attributes applied directly on native elements
  render() {
    return (
      <Host>
        <button
          class={this.hostClasses}
          disabled={this.disabled}
          aria-disabled={this.disabled ? "true" : undefined}
          onClick={() => !this.disabled && this.borealClick.emit()}
        >
          <slot name="icon" />
          <slot />
        </button>
      </Host>
    );
  }
}
```

**All properties must be optional** ‚Äî every `@Prop()` has a sensible default so the component works with zero configuration.

**Event naming convention:** All `@Event()` names must follow the pattern `bds{Action}` ‚Äî for example `bdsChange`, `bdsInput`, `bdsClick`. Do not include the component noun in the event name. The single exception is `valueChange`, which must remain generic because it is the framework integration contract consumed by the Vue output target for `v-model` two-way binding.

**Light DOM component root selector:** Use `:host` as the root CSS selector for all form controls and interactive components (buttons, tabs). Use a BEM root class on an inner `<div>` for layout and feedback components (banner, card, modal). The deciding factor is whether the component has browser-managed states reflected as attributes or pseudo-classes on the host element itself. Form controls require `:host` because `[disabled]` is a reflected attribute and `:focus-visible` / `:hover` must cascade outward from the element ‚Äî selectors like `bds-checkbox[disabled] .bds-checkbox__box` and `bds-checkbox:focus-visible .bds-checkbox__box` cannot be written without `:host` as the root. In Stencil light DOM, `:host([disabled])` compiles to `bds-checkbox[disabled] { ... }` and is globally scoped to the tag name.

To decide whether a prop needs `reflect: true`, apply this rule: use `reflect: true` only when:

1. The prop value is referenced in a CSS `:host([prop])` attribute selector in the component SCSS
2. The prop must remain observable as an HTML attribute at runtime (e.g. `disabled`)

### State Management

Use `@State()` for internal state that triggers re-renders but is not part of the public API:

```tsx
import { State } from '@stencil/core';

@State() private isOpen: boolean = false;
@State() private activeIndex: number = 0;
```

Never expose `@State()` properties publicly. If a consumer needs to set the value, use a `@Prop()` with `@Watch()` to sync it into private state.

```tsx
@Prop() open: boolean = false;
@State() private internalOpen: boolean = false;

@Watch('open')
onOpenChange(next: boolean) {
  this.internalOpen = next;
}

componentWillLoad() {
  this.internalOpen = this.open;
}
```

**`disabled` on FACE components must never use `@Prop({ mutable: true })`**. Stencil warns `@Prop() "disabled" should not be mutable` because `disabled` is a native reflected attribute with browser-managed semantics controlled via `formDisabledCallback`. Marking it `mutable: true` creates two writers on the same reflected attribute (the component and the browser), which can race. The correct pattern is a `@State()` mirror:

```tsx
@Prop({ reflect: true }) readonly disabled: boolean = false;
@State() private isDisabled: boolean = false;

@Watch('disabled')
onDisabledChange(next: boolean): void {
  this.isDisabled = next;
}

componentWillLoad(): void {
  this.isDisabled = this.disabled;
}

formDisabledCallback(disabled: boolean): void {
  this.isDisabled = disabled;
}
```

All render and toggle logic reads `this.isDisabled`. `@Prop()` stays `readonly` and externally owned.

### Prop Validation

For any enum-typed `@Prop()` (variant, size, type, color, etc.), use the shared `validatePropValue` utility combined with stacked `@Watch()` decorators on a single `checkPropValues()` method. Always call `checkPropValues()` in `componentWillLoad()` ‚Äî `@Watch()` alone does not fire for the initial attribute value set before the component mounts; without the `componentWillLoad()` call, an invalid attribute on the initial HTML render is silently accepted.

```tsx
import { Component, Element, Prop, Watch, h } from "@stencil/core";
import { validatePropValue } from "@/utils/helpers/validateProps";
import { BUTTON_VARIANTS, BUTTON_SIZES } from "./types/enum";
import { ButtonVariant, ButtonSize } from "./types/types";

@Component({ tag: "bds-button", styleUrl: "bds-button.scss" })
export class BdsButton {
  @Element() el!: HTMLBdsButtonElement;

  @Prop() readonly variant: ButtonVariant = "default";
  @Prop() readonly size: ButtonSize = "medium";

  @Watch("variant")
  @Watch("size")
  checkPropValues(): void {
    validatePropValue(Object.values(BUTTON_VARIANTS) as ButtonVariant[], "default", this.el, "variant");
    validatePropValue(Object.values(BUTTON_SIZES) as ButtonSize[], "medium", this.el, "size");
  }

  componentWillLoad(): void {
    this.checkPropValues();
  }
}
```

**`validatePropValue` signature** (source: `src/utils/helpers/validateProps.ts`):

```ts
validatePropValue<T extends string>(
  acceptedValues: readonly T[],
  fallbackValue: T,
  element: HTMLElement,
  propertyName: string,
): void
```

When the current prop value is not in `acceptedValues`, the utility resets `element[propertyName]` to `fallbackValue` and issues a `console.warn` naming the component tag, the invalid value, and the full list of valid options. This is a **mutation strategy** ‚Äî after `checkPropValues()` returns, all validated props are guaranteed to hold a valid value, and the rest of the component render path can proceed without defensive guards.

**Rules:**

- Always use `Object.values(ENUM)` as the accepted values source so the check stays in sync with the enum automatically.
- Prefer passing `this.el as HTMLElement` (not `this.el as HTMLBdsButtonElement`) to keep `validatePropValue` generic.
- Do not inline the valid-values array as a literal ‚Äî enum values are the single source of truth.
- `checkPropValues()` belongs in the **Property Watchers** section (section 6 of the member ordering) because it carries `@Watch()` decorators.

### Service Layer Architecture

The "service layer" in this design system is the **design token and utility layer** in `packages/boreal-web-components/src/utils/`. It provides:

- **Token utilities** ‚Äî functions that map prop values to token variable names
- **Validation helpers** ‚Äî input sanitisation and boundary checks
- **DOM helpers** ‚Äî cross-shadow-DOM queries, focus management, ID generation

All utility functions must be pure, explicitly typed, and unit-tested. Never import utility code from `apps/boreal-docs` into a publishable package.

---

## UI/UX Standards

### Design Token Integration

CSS custom properties (`var(--boreal-*)`) are the only styling mechanism for multi-brand theming. The token system has three layers ‚Äî always reference the correct layer:

| Layer              | Purpose                       | Example                                           | May reference     |
| ------------------ | ----------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------- | ----------------- |
| **Primitive**      | Raw palette values            | `--boreal-cobalt-40: #0066CC`                     | Nothing           |
| **Theme**          | Semantic roles per brand      | `--boreal-primary-base: var(--boreal-cobalt-40)`  | Primitives only   |
| **Usage/Semantic** | Component consumption aliases | `--boreal-bg-primary: var(--boreal-primary-base)` | Theme tokens only |

**Components reference Usage tokens only** ‚Äî never Primitive or Theme tokens directly.

In SCSS, always use CSS custom property references with a hard-coded fallback for token-free environments:

```scss
.boreal-button {
  background-color: var(--boreal-bg-primary, #0066cc);
  border-radius: var(--boreal-radius-xs, 4px);
  padding: var(--boreal-spacing-xs, 8px) var(--boreal-spacing-m, 16px);
  color: var(--boreal-text-on-primary, #ffffff);
}
```

Apply the `data-theme` attribute on `<html>` or `<body>` to switch brands. The component SCSS requires no theme-specific overrides ‚Äî the token sheet handles all brand differences automatically.

### Form Handling

Form-associated components (inputs, checkboxes, selects) must:

- Use the Stencil `@Component({ formAssociated: true })` option when native form participation is required
- Set `aria-required`, `aria-invalid`, and `aria-describedby` where applicable
- Emit a `change` event with `bubbles: true, composed: true` so forms detect value updates across shadow DOM
- Mirror the native `disabled` and `required` attributes via `@Prop({ reflect: true })`

For managing internal validation state, use `@State()` with a watch pattern that triggers only after the first user interaction (avoid showing errors on initial render).

**FACE mixin props rule:** `@Prop()` decorators declared inside a mixin factory are not picked up by the Stencil compiler. Props for `name`, `disabled`, and `required` must be declared directly on the component class ‚Äî not in the mixin. Mixins should carry **behavior only**: lifecycle callbacks (`formDisabledCallback`, `formResetCallback`, `formStateRestoreCallback`) and their method implementations. The `IFormControl<T>` interface still provides value by enforcing the shared contract across all FACE components via the `implements` clause.

### Slot-Based Composition Patterns

Prefer slots over props for any content that is itself HTML. Named slots provide structured compositional regions:

```tsx
render() {
  return (
    <Host>
      <div class="bds-card">
        <slot name="header" />          {/* Structured region */}
        <div class="bds-card__body">
          <slot />                      {/* Default slot for primary content */}
        </div>
        <slot name="footer" />
      </div>
    </Host>
  );
}
```

Use CSS Shadow Parts (`part="..."`) to expose styling hooks for consumer teams who need to override internal element styles without breaking encapsulation.

### Accessibility

Every component must meet WCAG AA requirements:

- Apply the correct ARIA role on the element that receives focus (prefer native HTML semantics first)
- Set `aria-disabled` (string `'true'`) in addition to the `disabled` attribute when disabling interactive elements
- Manage focus explicitly when a component opens an overlay: trap focus inside the overlay, restore focus on close
- Keyboard interactions must cover: `Tab` (focus traversal), `Enter`/`Space` (activation), `Escape` (dismiss), Arrow keys (navigation within a group)
- WCAG AA colour contrast: 4.5:1 minimum for text; 3:1 for large text and UI boundaries
- Test all stories with the Storybook a11y addon ‚Äî zero violations before merging

---

## Testing Standards

### Unit Testing with Stencil Spec Runner

All unit tests use `newSpecPage` from `@stencil/core/testing`. Follow these conventions:

```typescript
import { newSpecPage } from "@stencil/core/testing";
import { BdsButton } from "../bds-button";

describe("bds-button", () => {
  it("renders with default props", async () => {
    const { root } = await newSpecPage({
      components: [BdsButton],
      html: `<bds-button></bds-button>`,
    });
    expect(root).toEqualHtml(`
      <bds-button>
        <mock:shadow-root>
          <button class="bds-button bds-button--default bds-button--medium" type="button">
            ...
          </button>
        </mock:shadow-root>
      </bds-button>
    `);
  });

  it("renders as disabled when the disabled prop is true", async () => {
    const { root } = await newSpecPage({
      components: [BdsButton],
      html: `<bds-button disabled></bds-button>`,
    });
    const btn = root.shadowRoot.querySelector("button");
    expect(btn.disabled).toBe(true);
    expect(btn.getAttribute("aria-disabled")).toBe("true");
  });

  it("emits bdsClick when clicked", async () => {
    const { root, waitForChanges } = await newSpecPage({
      components: [BdsButton],
      html: `<boreal-button></boreal-button>`,
    });
    const handler = jest.fn();
    root.addEventListener("borealClick", handler);
    root.shadowRoot.querySelector("button").click();
    await waitForChanges();
    expect(handler).toHaveBeenCalledTimes(1);
  });
});
```

**Coverage requirements**: ‚â• 90% statement coverage. Run `pnpm --filter @telesign/boreal-web-components run test:spec` and check the coverage report in `coverage/`.

### FACE Component Test Setup

Every spec file for a component that uses `formAssociatedMixin` or declares `@AttachInternals()` must include this boilerplate:

```typescript
import {
  mockElementInternals,
  suppressElementInternalsErrors,
} from "@/utils/__test__";

let teardown: () => void;

beforeAll(() => {
  mockElementInternals();
  teardown = suppressElementInternalsErrors();
});

afterAll(() => {
  teardown();
});
```

`mockElementInternals()` polyfills `HTMLElement.prototype.attachInternals` for `newSpecPage`. `suppressElementInternalsErrors()` silences `console.error` output that Stencil's mock-doc generates on every `ElementInternals` property access ‚Äî optional-chain guards (`setFormValue?.()`) cannot prevent this because the getter fires on the property read itself, before the call decision.

Both utilities live in `packages/boreal-web-components/src/utils/__test__/mocks.ts`. Do not inline them in individual spec files and do not mix them into `helpers.ts`. The distinction is: `helpers.ts` holds assertion utilities and DOM query helpers; `mocks.ts` holds test doubles that replace real browser or runtime APIs.

### Test Organisation

#### Scaffolding: by functionality

Create different files for the following types of component functionality when applicable:

- A11y (Accessibility).
- Basics.
- Variants.
- Events.
- Slots.

The naming convention should follow the rule `{bds-component}.functionality.spec.tsx`. Example:

- `bds-component.a11y.spec.tsx`
- `bds-component.basics.spec.tsx`
- `bds-component.variants.spec.tsx`
- `bds-component.events.spec.tsx`
- `bds-component.slots.spec.tsx`

- One `describe` block per spec file
- One `it` per distinct behaviour (not per line of code)
- Test descriptions must read as specifications: `"renders as disabled when the disabled prop is true"` ‚Äî not `"disabled test"`
- Utility functions in `src/utils/` are tested separately with Vitest and must have their own `.spec.ts` files

---

## Configuration Standards

### TypeScript Configuration

Each package has its own `tsconfig.json` extending the root config. Key rules:

- `"strict": true` is the target for all new code
- `"noImplicitAny": false` is a legacy setting from the Stencil starter ‚Äî treat implicit `any` as an error regardless
- All exported public API surfaces must have explicit TypeScript types ‚Äî no inferred `any` in `.d.ts` output
- Use `interface` for object shapes, `type` for unions and mapped types

### ESLint Configuration

ESLint 9.x flat config (`eslint.config.ts`) with `typescript-eslint` and `eslint-config-prettier`. Key enforced rules:

- `@typescript-eslint/no-explicit-any` ‚Äî warning; treat as error in review
- `@typescript-eslint/explicit-function-return-type` ‚Äî required for exported functions
- No inline comments explaining what code does; use JSDoc on exported API surfaces only
- Prettier handles all formatting concerns; ESLint handles correctness rules only

**Mixin factory `any` suppression:** Mixin factories use `new (...args: any[]) => {}` as the base constructor type, which originates from Stencil's own `MixedInCtor` type. This can cause `@typescript-eslint/no-unsafe-argument` to fire on the implicit `super()` call. Do not add a no-op constructor to silence this ‚Äî a constructor that only calls `super(...args)` does nothing that JavaScript does not already do automatically, and it requires two `eslint-disable` comments to suppress the errors it introduces. The correct fix is a scoped suppression in `eslint.config.ts`:

```js
{
  files: ['**/mixins/**/*.ts'],
  rules: {
    '@typescript-eslint/no-unsafe-argument': 'off',
  },
}
```

This is honest ‚Äî the `any` originates from library types, not project code ‚Äî and it does not pollute the mixin source file.

### Environment Configuration

There are no environment variables in publishable packages. All visual configuration is handled through CSS custom properties applied via `data-theme` at the consumer level. Build-time configuration lives in:

- `stencil.config.ts` ‚Äî Stencil output targets and compiler options
- `turbo.json` ‚Äî Turborepo pipeline inputs, outputs, and caching
- `.release-it.json` (per package) ‚Äî release-it versioning and publishing config

---

## Performance Best Practices

### Component Optimisation

- **Minimise re-renders**: `@Prop()` changes cause re-renders; `@State()` changes cause re-renders. Derive computed values in getters, not `@State()`:

  ```tsx
  // ‚úÖ Good: getter derived when needed, no extra render cycle
  private get hostClasses(): string {
    return ['bds-badge', `bds-badge--${this.color}`].join(' ');
  }

  // ‚ùå Bad: updating @State triggers an extra render
  @Watch('color')
  onColorChange(color: string) {
    this.classes = `bds-badge bds-badge--${color}`;
  }
  ```

- **Use CSS `contain`** on components with complex internal layouts to limit browser layout recalculation scope.

- **Shadow DOM timing**: When a component needs to measure a DOM element after render, do it in `componentDidLoad()` or `componentDidUpdate()`. If timing issues arise between the JS event loop and rendering, defer with `requestAnimationFrame`.

### Bundle Optimisation

- Tree-shaking works automatically via the Stencil lazy-loading architecture ‚Äî each component is a separate chunk
- Never import an entire package when only one export is needed
- Avoid dynamic imports inside `render()` ‚Äî Stencil manages code splitting automatically

### API Efficiency

The design system has no network layer. "API efficiency" in this context means:

- CSS custom property lookups are O(1) ‚Äî prefer token-driven styling over computed prop values
- Slot detection (`hasSlotted`) should use `MutationObserver` or `slotchange` events, not polling
- Form validation logic should be debounced or gated behind `componentDidLoad()` to avoid flicker

---

## Development Workflow

### Git Workflow

| Branch                        | Purpose                                                                     |
| ----------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `main`                        | Stable, integrated code. All PRs target here.                               |
| `feature/[ticket]_bds-[name]` | New component or feature branches                                           |
| `fix/bds-[name]`              | Bug fix branches                                                            |
| `release/current`             | Release branch ‚Äî only `main` is merged here. Releases run from this branch. |

Always branch from `main`. Use `pnpm commit` (Commitizen) for all commits ‚Äî it enforces the conventional commit format required for automated versioning.

### Development Scripts

All scripts run from the **workspace root** via Turborepo:

| Goal                         | Command                                                       |
| ---------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------- |
| Build all packages           | `pnpm build`                                                  |
| Start Storybook              | `pnpm dev:docs`                                               |
| Start web components watcher | `pnpm dev:components`                                         |
| Run all tests                | `pnpm test`                                                   |
| Run component unit tests     | `pnpm --filter @telesign/boreal-web-components run test:spec` |
| Lint + typecheck all         | `pnpm turbo lint typecheck`                                   |
| Scaffold a new component     | `pnpm generate:component`                                     |
| Scaffold a new story         | `pnpm generate:story`                                         |
| Rebuild design tokens        | `pnpm rebuild:styles`                                         |
| Conventional commit          | `pnpm commit`                                                 |

### Code Quality

Pre-commit hooks (Husky + lint-staged) run ESLint and Prettier automatically.
The CI pipeline runs lint ‚Üí typecheck ‚Üí test ‚Üí build in sequence via Turborepo. All four jobs must pass before a PR can merge.

Quality bar before opening a PR:

1. `pnpm turbo lint typecheck` ‚Äî zero errors
2. `pnpm --filter @telesign/boreal-web-components run test:spec` ‚Äî ‚â• 90% coverage
3. Component renders correctly in `react-testapp` (`pnpm dev:components` + `pnpm --filter react-testapp dev`)
4. All Storybook stories pass the a11y addon (zero violations)
5. All commit messages authored via `pnpm commit`

---

## Migration Strategy

### Token Schema Evolution

When a design token is renamed or restructured in `boreal-styleguidelines`:

1. Add the new token name while keeping the old one as an alias pointing to the new value (deprecation period)
2. Update all component SCSS files to reference the new token name
3. Run `pnpm validate` in `boreal-styleguidelines` to confirm no dead references
4. Remove the deprecated alias in the next major version, with a migration note in the MDX documentation
5. Bump tracking: a token rename that changes component appearance is a `minor` change; a breaking removal is `major` (controlled by the conventional commit type on the implementing commit)

### Component API Evolution

When a component prop name, type, or event signature changes:

- **Additive change** (new optional prop, new event): `feat` commit ‚Üí minor bump
- **Non-breaking rename** (keep the old prop with `@deprecated` JSDoc, add the new prop): `feat` commit ‚Üí minor bump; remove the deprecated prop in the next major cycle
- **Breaking removal or type narrowing**: `feat!` or `BREAKING CHANGE` footer ‚Üí major bump; document the migration path in the MDX doc under a "Migration" section
- **Event rename** (e.g. `bdsCheckboxChange` ‚Üí `bdsChange`): this is always a breaking change. Consumers listening to the old event name will silently receive nothing. Requires a `feat!` commit, major bump, and a migration note.
- Framework wrappers (`boreal-react`, `boreal-vue`) inherit the same API automatically via output targets ‚Äî always verify wrappers after an API change by running `pnpm build` and checking the generated wrapper types in `packages/boreal-react/lib/components/`
